items.watch: critical-profile cost on ALL-OPUS routing — the priciest routing
items.status: *** HEADLINE FINDING ***  The opus-4-8/xhigh test-author ALONE drove the 5-hour rate window 0.01 -> 0.77 (~+0.75). The remaining critical pipeline is 5 MORE opus-4-8/xhigh workers (implementer @capable + 4-lens panel @max: correctness/repro/security/perf). The cumulative pipeline EXHAUSTED the 5h usage limit at the implementer boundary (the limit hit was hard enough to take down the Bash safety classifier). CONCLUSION: a single critical task's full 6-worker all-opus/xhigh pipeline does NOT fit in one 5-hour window (~0.75 window/worker). Critical tasks must be paced across multiple 5h windows; re-check the governor between EVERY stage. seven_day stayed healthy (0.03 -> ~0.25). The panel itself was not reached, but per-worker window cost is the decisive metric.

NONE. The test-author completed at 1474s, well under the 2700s deadline; no timed_out event. The 5h-limit hit was CUMULATIVE window exhaustion at the orchestrator/classifier level, NOT a worker wall-timeout or a per-worker 429. (Contrast leg-2 P3v2-13: a 38-min silent pre-compute hang — now structurally prevented by the wall deadline, though not exercised here.)

items.watch: parse_worker_result returns non-None on compliant workers (zero spurious ladder escalations)
items.status: OBSERVED + FINDING. Probe: parsed=dict cleanly. Test-author: parse_worker_result returned parsed=None on a CONTENT-COMPLIANT handoff because the worker PROSE-WRAPPED its valid JSON (prose before/around the object); parse_worker_result's single-fence tolerance strips one fenced block but not leading prose. Per the arm, parsed=None -> ladder — which would be a SPURIOUS escalation here. Orchestrator recovered via a string-aware balanced-brace extractor + schemas.validate_handoff; NO spurious escalation occurred (W19 goal met via recovery, not native parse). UPSTREAM FINDING: parse_worker_result should extract the largest balanced JSON object from `result` (tolerate prose-wrapped JSON), not only fenced JSON.
